text: The clean glass and structured stillness of the meeting room beckon to me with a kind of sterile anticipation, as though ideas are waiting for bodies to fill the chairs and bring them to life. In contrast, the second room bursts with color and the energy of invention—R+D, the restless pulse of progress, is made manifest on the wall and in the tangle of wires below the screens. I feel suspended between order and creative chaos, inspired yet quietly uneasy, pondering whether innovation thrives more in silence or in spectacle.
